---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

# 👋 Welcome
 
## ISSS608 – Visual Analytics Group Project Website

**Group Members: Cheok En Qi and Teong Sze Ching**

Singapore’s total fertility rate has fallen to a historic low, raising concerns about population ageing and the long-term sustainability of the labour force. Fertility trends are often shaped by a wider set of demographic and social factors, including marriage patterns, divorce trends, and delayed childbearing across different age groups.

Recent news coverage has highlighted Singapore’s declining fertility rate and the broader demographic challenges it raises. These include concerns about population ageing, workforce sustainability, delayed marriage, rising living costs, and changing life priorities, all of which may be linked to continued fertility decline.

Through this website and embedded Shiny app, our group explores how fertility has changed over time and examines how demographic indicators, particularly marriage and divorce, align with fertility patterns in Singapore.

Rather than focusing on prediction, this project emphasises exploratory and explanatory analysis. Specifically, it investigates how fertility patterns evolve over time, how marriage and divorce co-move with fertility across different lags in the overall yearly series, and how these relationships can be examined more formally using age-group-by-year panel data. The analytical components therefore focus on time-series visualisation, cross-correlation analysis and panel regression.

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r echo=FALSE, out.width="100%", out.height="350px", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics("data/article.jpg")
```

*Figure 1: News related to Singapore’s fertility trends.*
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r echo=FALSE, out.width="100%", out.height="350px", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics("data/family.jpg")
```

*Image source: Pixabay, royalty-free image.*
:::
:::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------
